Use cases: modern SaaS, AI app, dashboard, developer tool, settings page
Evidence: direct_spec / high
Source: https://ui.shadcn.com/

- clean
- composable
- neutral

- AI app
- dashboard
- developer tool
- settings page

Avoid when:

- luxury visual campaign

AI summary: Use shadcn/ui-style composition for modern React/Tailwind web apps: accessible primitives, clean neutral surfaces, consistent rhythm, and explicit component states.

source_name: Web-verified Liquid Glass CSS parameter ranges
content:
# Web-verified Liquid Glass CSS parameter ranges - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: CSS implementation, React UI, Tailwind UI, premium web app, Liquid Glass component system
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/backdrop-filter

- Light glass fill: rgba(255,255,255,0.18) to rgba(255,255,255,0.42); for thicker white glass use 0.45 to 0.68.
- Dark glass fill: rgba(8,10,16,0.56) to rgba(8,10,16,0.78); keep text white or near-white.
- Backdrop filter baseline: blur(12px) saturate(140%) for subtle glass; blur(18px) saturate(160%) for strong frosted glass; blur(24px) saturate(180%) for hero liquid glass.
- Border/rim: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.35) to rgba(255,255,255,0.68); add a lower dark inset edge rgba(20,30,50,0.10) to 0.22 for thickness.
- Radius: 18px to 28px for fields/cards; 999px for pill buttons; 32px to 44px for large modal cards.
- Outer shadow: 0 10px 30px rgba(31,38,135,0.12) for light components; 0 18px 48px rgba(20,30,60,0.22) for thick floating glass.
- Inner highlight: inset 0 1px 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.70), inset 0 -1px 2px rgba(20,30,60,0.12).
- Gloss pseudo-element: top-left white radial/linear gradient at 0.35 to 0.65 opacity, clipped inside the radius.
- Chromatic caustics: small radial gradients in cyan/magenta/orange at 0.18 to 0.36 opacity near edges or corners; do not cover text areas.
- Performance: avoid applying backdrop-filter to hundreds of cells/rows; use it on containers and key controls only.

AI summary: Liquid Glass CSS parameter ranges: use transparent fills, backdrop-filter blur(12-24px) saturate(140-180%), 1px white rim, large radii, layered outer/inset shadows, gloss pseudo-elements, chromatic caustics, and accessibility/performance fallbacks.

# Dark neon Liquid Glass implementation parameters - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium card, dark mode hero, selected state, Pro plan card, AI control panel, neon status tile

Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/dark-neon-liquid-glass-parameters

- Base dark glass: rgba(8,10,16,0.68) to rgba(8,10,16,0.78); strong default rgba(8,10,16,0.72).
- Secondary dark glass: rgba(15,23,42,0.58) to rgba(15,23,42,0.70).
- Backdrop filter: blur(18px) saturate(160%) for cards; blur(24px) saturate(180%) for hero/premium surfaces.
- White rim: border 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.18) to rgba(255,255,255,0.32); hover can rise to 0.42.
- Cyan glint: rgba(34,211,238,0.28) to rgba(34,211,238,0.46), usually radial-gradient at top-left or icon edge.
- Magenta glint: rgba(217,70,239,0.22) to rgba(217,70,239,0.40), usually bottom-right or active ring.
- Orange glint: rgba(251,146,60,0.16) to rgba(251,146,60,0.32), use sparingly for warm caustic highlights.
- Deep shadow: 0 24px 60px rgba(2,6,23,0.34) plus 0 8px 24px rgba(15,23,42,0.22).
- Inset gloss: inset 0 1px 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.22), inset 0 -1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.35).
- Neon active ring: box-shadow 0 0 0 1px rgba(34,211,238,0.38), 0 0 24px rgba(217,70,239,0.24).
- Pressed state: reduce translateY by 1px, increase inset dark shadow, and slightly reduce outer glow.
- Disabled state: background rgba(15,23,42,0.38), border rgba(255,255,255,0.12), text rgba(248,250,252,0.45), remove neon glints.

- applying backdrop-filter to hundreds of repeated rows

AI summary: For high-end Liquid Glass web interfaces, use a slow, layered ambient scene behind glass surfaces so blur and refraction have meaningful visual input. Prefer ecological scenery such as aurora haze, water caustics, drifting mist, star dust, city light reflections, or abstract data currents over static gradients or decorative blobs. Keep motion subtle, preserve legibility, and provide reduced-motion fallbacks.

- Add an overlay dimmer under modals so clear glass stays legible over media-rich scenery.
- Keep animation on transform and opacity where possible; avoid animating blur radius or expensive filters across large full-screen layers.
- Use CSS custom properties for scene hue, glow strength, glass opacity, and motion speed so the background can adapt per page.

AI summary: For high-end Liquid Glass web interfaces, use a slow, layered ambient scene behind glass surfaces so blur and refraction have meaningful visual input. # MDN SVG filter turbulence and displacement references - Overview

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: liquid glass interface background, water caustic lighting, refractive product UI, premium modal backdrop, high-end glass controls
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Reference/Element/feTurbulence

Visual traits:
- slow refractive water-light caustics behind glass
- thin flowing highlight veins that suggest optical distortion
- subtle cyan, silver, and warm reflected light
- liquid depth without turning the page into an aquarium
- background movement that makes glass edges feel optically active

Use when:
- liquid glass interface background
- water caustic lighting
- refractive product UI
- premium modal backdrop
- high-end glass controls

Avoid when:
- dense data tables
- long reading pages
- forms with many validation messages
- rapid water ripple effects
- high-contrast wave lines under text
- heavy SVG filters over full-screen layers on low-end devices

AI summary: Use water caustics when Liquid Glass needs a more optical, refractive background than a static gradient. Keep flowing light low-opacity, slow, and away from text; implement with SVG turbulence/displacement, low-res canvas noise, or CSS highlight layers, with reduced-motion and static fallbacks.

# MDN SVG filter turbulence and displacement references - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: liquid glass interface background, water caustic lighting, refractive product UI, premium modal backdrop, high-end glass controls
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Reference/Element/feTurbulence

- SVG baseline: use feTurbulence as a procedural noise source and feDisplacementMap or masked gradients for slow refractive distortion.
- Canvas baseline: render low-resolution flowing noise or line fields, upscale softly, and keep opacity low.
- CSS fallback: layer radial gradients and thin blurred linear highlights with long transform animations.
- Throttle intensity on mobile and avoid animating large blur/filter values every frame.
- Use requestAnimationFrame for canvas animation and stop or pause when the page is hidden.
- Keep caustic animation loops slow, around 24s to 90s, with no visible reset.

AI summary: Use water caustics when Liquid Glass needs a more optical, refractive background than a static gradient. Keep flowing light low-opacity, slow, and away from text; implement with SVG turbulence/displacement, low-res canvas noise, or CSS highlight layers, with reduced-motion and static fallbacks.
